definition: value as specified by level (miNomax) of the insertion loss of a delay line

definition_ml.en: value as specified by level (miNomax) of the insertion loss of a delay line

remark: Insertion loss |The ratio, generally expressed in decibels, of the power at a point in a transmission path to the power at the same point after insertion of a two-port electrical network into the transmission path ahead of this point. |NOTE - If the ratio defining the insertion loss is less than one, its decibel value is negative, and its converse or opposite decibel value called 'insertion gain' may be used.

remark_ml.en: Insertion loss |The ratio, generally expressed in decibels, of the power at a point in a transmission path to the power at the same point after insertion of a two-port electrical network into the transmission path ahead of this point. |NOTE - If the ratio defining the insertion loss is less than one, its decibel value is negative, and its converse or opposite decibel value called 'insertion gain' may be used.
